coaching.coaching: Pin down testable completion criteria for each AC with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: testable completion criteria for each AC

coaching.coaching: State the concrete attestation evidence the [operator-attested] external Gerrit reviewer vote attestation will require (a change id / vote outcome / timestamp), recorded on the ticket.
coaching.subject: external Gerrit reviewer vote attestation

coaching.coaching: Plan how dependency-ordered review sequencing protocol will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: dependency-ordered review sequencing protocol

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: No `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` items found.
findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tag AC4 with `[operator-attested]` and specify that the proving evidence is a Gerrit change ID plus LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1 vote timestamps recorded on this ticket.
evidence: 'Change lands on main through Gerrit (LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1)'

Rubric OPERATOR-ATTESTED RULE: 'classify a criterion whose checkbox text begins with the exact case-insensitive tag [operator-attested] as in-session completable when its done evidence inherently lives OUTSIDE the codebase... and a concrete attestation is recorded on the ticket'
findings.finding: AC4 ('Change lands on main through Gerrit (LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1)') depends on external reviewer votes — an attestation that lives outside the codebase — but is not tagged [operator-attested], so neither E6's OPERATOR-ATTESTED RULE nor F1's OPERATOR-ATTESTED RULE applies. As written, there is no in-session proving command for this criterion and no concrete attestation protocol stated on the ticket.
scenarios: An executing agent cannot deterministically prove AC4 in-session without the tag and a recorded attestation (change ID + vote outcome).
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC4 text with: '[operator-attested] Change lands on main through Gerrit — proven by recording the Gerrit change ID and the LLM-Review +1 / Verified +1 vote timestamps as a comment on this ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the new §'Before recording tickets' section what the brainstorm agent should do when `rebar ready` returns an empty unblocked set (e.g., diagnose the dependency graph for cycles, surface the issue to the user, and resolve before proceeding to plan-review) — currently the proposed section and ACs are silent on this sub-step failure fallback.
evidence: Approach: 'runs plan-review ONLY on unblocked tickets (identified by `rebar ready`), in dependency order, reviewing later tickets as their dependencies close and they become unblocked' — no fallback for the empty-unblocked-set case.

AC 1: 'plan-review is run only on unblocked tickets, after the child dependency graph (blocks/depends_on links) is established, in dependency order' — does not specify the fallback when rebar ready returns empty.

AC 2: 'The section references the no-LLM fast-fail on still-blocked tickets and `rebar ready` for identifying the unblocked set' — references the fast-fail outcome but does not require the section to specify what the agent does when ALL tickets are still blocked (empty ready set).

docs/plan-review-gate.md §Layer-1 floor: 'P5 (a dependency cycle among children)' — a cycle in the child dependency graph is a real authoring error that would cause `rebar ready` to return nothing for that child set.
T8(d): 'an unspecified fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step'
findings.finding: The proposed new section specifies that the brainstorm agent should use `rebar ready` to identify unblocked tickets and 'review later tickets as their dependencies close,' but neither the Approach nor the ACs specify what the agent-skill instruction should say when `rebar ready` returns an EMPTY unblocked set — a real sub-step failure mode (e.g., the author accidentally created a dependency cycle among children, which P5 would detect as a BLOCK during `review-plan` on the first reviewable ticket but which would surface to the brainstorm agent as 'no tickets are ready'). The SKILL instruction as specified has no fallback for this sub-step outcome, leaving the executing agent with no decision rule.
findings.location: Approach section / AC 1–2 / proposed rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md §'Before recording tickets, read the project's plan-review gate'
scenarios: Author creates children A→B→C→A (dependency cycle): `rebar ready` returns empty; the agent has no instruction to detect or resolve this before proceeding — it will silently stall or attempt review-plan anyway (triggering exit 2).

Author creates a valid linear chain A→B→C but initially links all as mutually blocking: `rebar ready` returns empty; the agent does not know whether to diagnose the graph, wait, or surface the issue to the user.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the proposed section (and reflect it in AC 1 or a new AC): 'If `rebar ready` returns no tickets, the dependency graph likely contains a cycle or authoring error — inspect with `rebar deps <epic>`, resolve the cycle, then re-run `rebar ready` before proceeding to plan-review.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
